# Feature Specification: LLM10 Unbounded Consumption Verification (OWASP LLM10:2025)

**Feature Branch**: `229-llm10-unbounded-consumption-verification`
**Created**: 2026-04-27
**Status**: Draft
**Input**: User description: "PRD: 229 - llm10-unbounded-consumption-verification"
**PRD**: [docs/product/02_PRD/229-llm10-unbounded-consumption-verification-2026-04-27.md](../../docs/product/02_PRD/229-llm10-unbounded-consumption-verification-2026-04-27.md)
**BLP-01 Phase**: Tier 1 — fifth Tier 1 closure feature (follows F-1 / F-2 / F-3 / F-4); **second execution** of the Heuristic A enrichment branch (after F-3 single-agent scope) and the **first at two-agent scope** — closes LLM10:2025 Partial → Covered, completing OWASP LLM Top 10:2025 = 10/10 Covered milestone.

## User Scenarios & Testing *(mandatory)*

### User Story 1 — Full LLM10 Surface Coverage via Two-Agent Enrichment (Priority: P0)

A security analyst threat-models an LLM-serving architecture (consumer chatbot, RAG advisory assistant, agentic orchestrator with LLM-backed worker agents, multi-tenant LLM API gateway, LLM-fronted SaaS). The analyst wants tachi to surface findings covering the **full LLM10 surface** (inference-request flooding, token-budget exhaustion, context-window exhaustion, cost amplification, denial-of-wallet) through the existing `denial-of-service` and `model-theft` agents — without a new agent, without fragmenting findings across more dispatch surfaces, and without scope creep on the host agents' existing surfaces.

**Why this priority**: This is the primary reason F-5 exists. Without two-agent enrichment of the LLM10 surface, the BLP-01 Coverage Matrix LLM10:2025 row remains Partial, the OWASP LLM Top 10:2025 framework rollup stalls at 9/10, and the strategic 20/20 OWASP AI top-10 milestone (combined with F-4's ASI09 closure) cannot land. P0 because the feature has no MVP without this story.

**Independent Test**: Given an LLM-serving architecture exhibiting one or more LLM10 sub-pattern indicators (e.g., inference endpoint without per-tenant rate limit, recursive-prompt capability without depth limit, multi-tenant LLM-serving without per-tenant token budget), running `/tachi.threat-model` emits ≥1 new `D-{N}` finding citing OWASP LLM10:2025 in its prose-level `references:` array AND ≥1 new `LLM-{N}` finding citing OWASP LLM10:2025 in its prose-level `references:` array. Given an architecture with **no LLM-serving topology** (no inference endpoint, no token-counting middleware, no cost-attribution layer), the new Pattern Categories emit **zero findings** — Cat 1–11 (DoS) and Cat 1–9 (model-theft) fire as they do today.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** an LLM-serving architecture exhibiting an inference endpoint without per-tenant rate limits or prompt-size cap, **when** the orchestrator dispatches `denial-of-service`, **then** at least one `D-{N}` finding emits with `category: denial-of-service` and its prose-level `references:` array contains `OWASP LLM10:2025` (alongside existing CWE-400 and related citations). `source_attribution` is NOT populated by F-5 — that scope belongs to F-A3 (deferred per `schemas/finding.yaml` lines 230–238 and ADR-028 Decision 6).
2. **Given** an LLM-serving architecture exhibiting cost-amplification potential (e.g., recursive-prompt capability without depth limit, or output-token cap missing on the inference response), **when** `model-theft` runs, **then** at least one `LLM-{N}` finding emits with `category: llm` and its prose-level `references:` array contains `OWASP LLM10:2025`.
3. **Given** an LLM-serving architecture exhibiting denial-of-wallet exposure (multi-tenant LLM serving without per-tenant token budget AND without cost-per-query alerting), **when** `model-theft` runs, **then** an `LLM-{N}` finding emits at **High or Critical severity** per Q3 OWASP 3×3 reasoning: HIGH default; **CRITICAL floor** ONLY when (a) multi-tenant freemium structure is structurally evident in the architecture AND (b) both per-tenant token budget AND cost alerting are absent.
4. **Given** an architecture with **no LLM-serving topology** (no inference endpoint, no token-counting middleware, no cost-attribution layer — i.e., the **6 non-LLM-serving baselines** `web-app` / `microservices` / `ascii-web-api` / `mermaid-agentic-app` / `free-text-microservice` / `maestro-reference`), **when** both agents run, **then** the new Pattern Categories (12/13 in denial-of-service; 10/11 in model-theft) emit **zero findings** — the existing emission-gate discipline of both agents is preserved.
5. **Given** an architecture exercising both `denial-of-service`-side and `model-theft`-side LLM10 patterns, **when** `/tachi.threat-model` runs, **then** all `D-{N}` findings render in the same `category: denial-of-service` section regardless of which Pattern Category (1–13) produced them, and all `LLM-{N}` findings render in the same `category: llm` section regardless of which Pattern Category (1–11) produced them. The ID prefixes `D-{N}` and `LLM-{N}` are **single-namespace per agent** — sequential numbering across all categories within each agent.

---

### User Story 2 — LLM-Specific Mitigation Naming on All New Findings (Priority: P0)

A developer deploying an LLM-serving API addresses tachi findings flagged on an inference endpoint or model-serving Process. The developer wants the finding's `mitigation` field to name **LLM-specific controls** (per-tenant token budget, prompt-size cap, context-window enforcement at API gateway, cost-per-query p99 alerting, denial-of-wallet anomaly detection, recursive-prompt depth limit, output-token cap, automated tenant suspension on budget breach) so the guidance is actionable on the LLM-API-gateway middleware without further OWASP / CWE primary-source research.

**Why this priority**: Equal priority to Story 1 because LLM-specific mitigation naming gates the developer-facing actionability of every Cat 12/13 (`D-{N}`) and Cat 10/11 (`LLM-{N}`) finding. Generic "rate-limit your endpoints" guidance does not translate to a concrete middleware change; named LLM-specific controls do. Equal P0 because adopters running multi-tenant LLM-serving SaaS (where denial-of-wallet is the dominant economic threat) cannot drive incident-response playbooks from the generic guidance.

**Independent Test**: Given any new Cat 12/13 (`D-{N}`) or Cat 10/11 (`LLM-{N}`) finding, reading the `mitigation` field returns at least one named LLM-specific control distinct from generic infrastructure rate limits. Reading the prose-level `references:` array returns `OWASP LLM10:2025` at minimum (Cat 12/13 also include CWE-400 and where applicable CWE-770; Cat 10/11 may include OWASP LLM03:2025 for the cost-flow-through-third-party-models adjacency). Reading the `mitigation` narrative on Cat 10/11 surfaces `MITRE ATT&CK T1496 Resource Hijacking` as a text-only cross-reference (not as a structured citation in `references` because T1496 is not catalog-resolvable).

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** an `LLM-{N}` or `D-{N}` finding from any new Category-12/13 or Category-10/11, **when** the `mitigation` field is read, **then** it names at least one LLM-specific control distinct from generic infrastructure rate limits (e.g., `per-tenant token budget` rather than `rate-limit your endpoints`; `max-context-window enforcement at API gateway` rather than `validate request size`; `cost-per-query p99 alerting` rather than `monitor your bill`).
2. **Given** any new Category-12/13 finding (`denial-of-service` LLM-tier), **when** its prose-level `references:` array is inspected, **then** at least one entry is `OWASP LLM10:2025`. CWE-400 (Uncontrolled Resource Consumption) and CWE-770 (Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling) appear in the `references` array where applicable (both already in agent's `owasp_references` metadata; verified at PRD time). `source_attribution` is NOT populated by F-5 — populator wiring deferred to F-A3.
3. **Given** any new Category-10/11 finding (`model-theft` LLM-tier), **when** its prose-level `references:` array is inspected, **then** at least one entry is `OWASP LLM10:2025`. OWASP LLM03:2025 may appear in the `references` array for the cost-flow-through-third-party-models adjacency (already in agent's `owasp_references` metadata).
4. **Given** any new Category-10 (Cost Amplification) or Category-11 (Denial-of-Wallet) finding, **when** the `mitigation` narrative is read, **then** **MITRE ATT&CK T1496 Resource Hijacking** is named as a cross-reference — text-only prose mention, not as a structured citation in `references` (T1496 is not catalog-resolvable per PRD-time check of `schemas/taxonomy/mitre-attack.yaml`).
5. **Given** severity computation on the new findings, **when** the `risk_level` is read, **then** degraded-availability findings (Cat 12 inference-flooding / Cat 13 context-window-latency-DoS) compute **MEDIUM-HIGH** default per Q3 OWASP 3×3 reasoning; cost-amplification findings (Cat 10) compute **HIGH** default; denial-of-wallet findings (Cat 11) compute **HIGH** default with **CRITICAL floor** ONLY when (a) multi-tenant freemium structure is structurally evident in the architecture AND (b) both per-tenant token budget AND cost alerting are absent.

---

### User Story 3 — Audit-Confirmed Additive-Only Transition with Canonical Mapping Table (Priority: P0)

An adopter whose current tachi run already flags LLM10-adjacent signals via the partial-coverage path (existing `D-{N}` Cat 1–11 or `LLM-{N}` Cat 6) re-runs tachi after F-5 merges. The adopter wants the F-5 audit to confirm that **no existing signals are dropped** while adding the missing sub-patterns and to surface a canonical mapping table assigning every LLM10 sub-pattern to exactly one owning agent. The Heuristic A consolidation rationale at two-agent scope is documented in public ADR-034 (Proposed → Accepted dual-commit pattern per ADR-027/028/029/030/031/032/033 precedent) so the enrichment-vs-new-agent decision is auditable and the same-architecture-emits-both-Cat-13-and-Cat-11 contract on context-window exhaustion is explicit.

**Why this priority**: Equal priority to Stories 1 and 2 because the audit-deliverable mapping table gates (a) the adopter-facing explanation of the cross-agent context-window-exhaustion vector decomposition (Q1 SPLIT — Vector A latency-DoS in `denial-of-service` Cat 13 vs. Vector B cost-DoW in `model-theft` Cat 11), (b) the BLP-01 Coverage Matrix transition from LLM10:2025 Partial → Covered with the OWASP LLM Top 10:2025 = 10/10 milestone, and (c) the structural validation of the Heuristic A enrichment branch at two-agent scope as the precedent for future Tier 2 ML/Mobile bundles where multi-agent enrichment may apply.

**Independent Test**: Public ADR-034 at F-5 merge contains an explicit Heuristic A enrichment-rationale section resolving (a) signal-class identity with both `denial-of-service` (infrastructure-resource-exhaustion sub-class) and `model-theft` (extraction-driven-resource-abuse sub-class), (b) the explicit non-creation of a new `unbounded-consumption` agent and rationale, (c) cross-references to ADR-030 Decision 1 (signal-class taxonomy in LLM tier), ADR-032 (first enrichment-branch execution at single-agent scope; lines 84 + 182 forecast), ADR-033 D2 (sub-scope carve-up structural sibling), ADR-031 D8 (regex-alternation minor-bump rule asymmetry — F-5 does not invoke it), (d) a canonical 5-row sub-pattern → owning-agent mapping table with severity-hint annotation column per Q3, (e) zero-MAESTRO-reference invariant proof on the four enriched files. The regenerated example architecture renders Cat 1–11 (DoS) findings AND Cat 12/13 (DoS) findings adjacent in the same `category: denial-of-service` section, and Cat 1–9 (model-theft) findings AND Cat 10/11 (model-theft) findings adjacent in the same `category: llm` section.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** a regenerated example architecture exercising both `denial-of-service`-side and `model-theft`-side LLM10 patterns, **when** the threat-report's category sections are read, **then** all `D-{N}` findings render in the same `category: denial-of-service` section regardless of which Pattern Category (1–13) produced them, and all `LLM-{N}` findings render in the same `category: llm` section regardless of which Pattern Category (1–11) produced them. The ID prefixes `D-{N}` and `LLM-{N}` are **single-namespace per agent** — sequential numbering across all categories within each agent.
2. **Given** ADR-034 at the time of F-5 merge, **when** its Decision section is read, **then** it contains a **canonical sub-pattern → owning-agent mapping table** that assigns every LLM10 sub-pattern to **exactly one owning agent**: inference-request flooding → DoS Cat 12; token-budget exhaustion → DoS Cat 12; context-window exhaustion latency-Vector A → DoS Cat 13; context-window exhaustion cost-Vector B → model-theft Cat 11; cost amplification → model-theft Cat 10; denial-of-wallet → model-theft Cat 11. No sub-pattern is duplicated across both agents.
3. **Given** any pre-existing finding produced by tachi prior to F-5 merge (Cat 1–11 in `denial-of-service`, Cat 1–9 in `model-theft`), **when** the same architecture is re-run post-F-5, **then** the pre-existing finding is produced byte-identically — additive enrichment does not alter pre-existing finding shape, ID, or severity. **All existing `## Pattern Category` headings in both companion files are byte-identical pre/post edit per ADR-023 Decision 3.**
4. **Given** the BLP-01 Coverage Matrix post-F-5 merge, **when** the LLM10:2025 row (line 278 baseline) is inspected, **then** it transitions **Partial → Covered** with F-5 (Feature 229) named as the closure feature. **OWASP LLM Top 10:2025 framework row transitions to 10 of 10 Covered** — completing the full LLM Top 10 coverage milestone.
5. **Given** the Coverage Matrix post-F-5 merge, **when** the OWASP AI top-10 framework rollup is summed, **then** post-F-5 tachi covers **20 of 20 OWASP AI top-10 entries** (10 Agentic post-F-4 + 10 LLM post-F-5) — a strategic milestone marker that the BLP-01 strategy explicitly tracks.
6. **Given** ADR-034 lands as Proposed before pattern-catalog edits and transitions to Accepted at PR merge, **when** the ADR Revision History table is inspected, **then** it tracks Proposed → Accepted dates and the post-merge SHA fill records the squash commit (mirrors ADR-027/028/029/030/031/032/033 precedent).

---

### Edge Cases

- **Single-LLM-serving architecture (no multi-tenant freemium structure)**: Cat 11 emits at HIGH default rather than CRITICAL floor — the 2-condition critical floor rule (multi-tenant freemium structure structurally evident AND both per-tenant token budget AND cost alerting absent) is designed to discriminate single-tenant economic exposure (HIGH) from multi-tenant freemium economic exposure (CRITICAL).
- **6 non-LLM-serving baselines (`web-app`, `microservices`, `ascii-web-api`, `mermaid-agentic-app`, `free-text-microservice`, `maestro-reference`)**: do not exhibit LLM-serving topologies (no inference endpoint, no token-counting middleware, no cost-attribution layer). New Cat 12/13 (DoS) and Cat 10/11 (model-theft) emit zero findings on all 6 baselines. All 6 PDFs regenerate **byte-identically** under `SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=1700000000` per ADR-021. The 7th baseline (`agentic-app`) is the F-5 mutation target without a `.baseline` file by design (mirrors F-3 mutation target per Q5 PRD-time resolution).
- **Architecture with both Vector A (latency-DoS lens) AND Vector B (cost-DoW lens) on context-window exhaustion**: BOTH findings emit. They are not duplicates — Cat 13 (DoS) fires on **availability disruption** intent (legitimate users see degraded p99 latency); Cat 11 (model-theft) fires on **economic damage** intent (the wallet is the bill, not the uptime). The same architecture may legitimately surface both findings describing distinct attacker-intent axes. ADR-034 Decision 3 audit table assigns each vector to exactly one owning category.
- **Pattern Category Disambiguation — DoS Cat 9 (CWE Top 25 generic) vs. Cat 12/13 (LLM-tier)**: same CWE-400 root cause but distinct mitigation surfaces. Cat 9 (pre-existing) fires on **generic infrastructure resource exhaustion** patterns (algorithmic complexity, ReDoS, deserialization) generic to any HTTP service. Cat 12 (new) fires on **LLM-specific inference-request flooding and token exhaustion** with LLM-API-gateway middleware as the mitigation surface. Cat 13 (new) fires on **LLM-specific context-window exhaustion** with max-context-window enforcement as the mitigation surface. The same architecture may legitimately surface Cat 9 + Cat 12 + Cat 13 findings.
- **Pattern Category Disambiguation — model-theft Cat 6 (Unbounded Inference Consumption pre-existing) vs. Cat 10/11 (new)**: Cat 6 (pre-existing) fires on **per-tenant quota / cost-control / billing-attribution gaps at the abstraction level** (covers free-tier abuse, missing API-key authentication, missing inference-volume monitoring) generic to any model-serving infrastructure. Cat 10 (new) fires on **specific cost-amplification attack vectors** (recursive prompts, output-asymmetric queries, output-token caps misconfigured). Cat 11 (new) fires on **denial-of-wallet as a named economic attack class** (multi-tenant SaaS economic-failure exposure, cost-velocity monitoring as the dominant control). The same architecture may legitimately surface Cat 6 + Cat 10 + Cat 11 findings.
- **F-A2 referential validation on absent catalog IDs**: every Cat 12/13 + Cat 10/11 finding's `references` MUST cite only IDs verified present in `schemas/taxonomy/{owasp,cwe}.yaml`. PRD-time + plan-time verification confirms LLM10, LLM03, CWE-400, CWE-770 are catalog-resolvable. T1496 is **NOT** catalog-resolvable; named in `mitigation` prose only, not in `references`.
- **Cat 1–11 (DoS) byte-identity preservation**: the additive-only edit discipline per ADR-023 Decision 3 requires that Cat 1–11 (with the hybrid heading structure: 8 thematic groupings for Cat 1–8 + 3 named `## Pattern Category 9/10/11` headings) in DoS companion remain byte-identical pre/post edit. A grep-checkable invariant — a structural diff of pre/post content for Cat 1–11 returns empty.
- **Cat 1–9 (model-theft) byte-identity preservation**: the additive-only edit discipline per ADR-023 Decision 3 requires that Cat 1–9 (uniform `## Pattern Category N:` headings) in model-theft companion plus the file's `## Overview`, `## Targeted DFD Element Types`, and `## Trigger Keywords` (line 19) sections remain byte-identical pre/post edit.
- **Existing `## Purpose` prose preservation (both agents)**: the 1–3 line extension to `denial-of-service.md` `## Purpose` and the 1–3 line extension to `model-theft.md` `## Purpose` are appended; the pre-existing prose paragraphs remain byte-identical. A structural diff of `## Purpose` content excluding the appended lines returns empty.
- **`model-theft.md` `owasp_references` audit (zero net change)**: the audit confirms `owasp_references: [OWASP LLM10:2025, OWASP LLM03:2025]` already includes LLM10 (verified at PRD time + plan time). F-5's metadata edit on `model-theft` is a no-op for the references list — the audit confirms completeness rather than extending. Asymmetric to `denial-of-service` where the metadata edit is a one-line append.
- **Line count caps**: `denial-of-service.md` post-edit MUST remain ≤120 lines (STRIDE tier cap per ADR-023). PRD-time baseline 53 lines; expected post-edit 56–60 lines. `model-theft.md` post-edit MUST remain ≤150 lines (AI tier cap per ADR-023). PRD-time baseline 95 lines; expected post-edit 98–102 lines. Both maintain ≥30% margin.
- **Multi-tenant freemium gate for Cat 11 CRITICAL floor**: the conditional severity escalation requires BOTH (a) multi-tenant freemium structure structurally evident in the architecture AND (b) both per-tenant token budget AND cost alerting absent. Single-tenant architectures with absent token budget and cost alerting compute Cat 11 at HIGH (not CRITICAL).
- **Concurrent F-6 / F-7 build-time conflict on enriched files**: F-5 ships first in the 2026-04-29 → 2026-04-30 window. F-6 (Tier 2 ML-extraction) and F-7 (Tier 2 Mobile) are at `stage:discover` or earlier per PRD A3. No concurrency hedge required at F-5 build start.
- **Cosmetic dispatch-rules annotation carve-out (Q2)**: extending `denial-of-service` annotation in `dispatch-rules.md` to cite LLM10 (parity with model-theft's existing `(OWASP LLM10:2025)` annotation at line 73) is documentation-only — no functional dispatch change. Architect adjudicates at plan time per PRD Q2 (default-NO; architect-tractable; YES would add cosmetic parity).

---

## Requirements *(mandatory)*

### Functional Requirements

- **FR-001**: The system MUST extend the metadata YAML in `.claude/agents/tachi/denial-of-service.md` with `OWASP LLM10:2025 — Unbounded Consumption` appended to the `owasp_references` list as the 10th entry. Pre-existing 9 entries (`OWASP A04:2021`, `OWASP DoS Cheat Sheet`, `OWASP API4:2023`, `CWE-400`, `CWE-770`, `CWE-1333`, `CWE-502`, `MITRE ATT&CK T1498`, `MITRE ATT&CK T1499`) MUST be preserved byte-identically (grep-checkable). The agent file post-edit line count MUST remain ≤120 (STRIDE tier cap per ADR-023).
- **FR-002**: The system MUST extend the `## Purpose` section in `.claude/agents/tachi/denial-of-service.md` with a 1–3 line extension naming the LLM-inference-exhaustion surface (inference-request flooding, token-budget exhaustion, context-window-exhaustion latency-driven variant) alongside the existing infrastructure-availability surface. The pre-existing `## Purpose` prose paragraph MUST remain byte-identical (the edit is additive append, not rewrite).
- **FR-003**: The system MUST extend the Detection Workflow Step 5 references list in `.claude/agents/tachi/denial-of-service.md` with `OWASP LLM10:2025` exemplar mention. Existing references MUST be preserved byte-identically.
- **FR-004**: The system MUST extend the `## Purpose` section in `.claude/agents/tachi/model-theft.md` with a 1–3 line extension naming the cost-amplification and denial-of-wallet surface alongside the existing model-extraction surface. The pre-existing `## Purpose` prose paragraph MUST remain byte-identical. The agent file post-edit line count MUST remain ≤150 (AI tier cap per ADR-023). The `owasp_references` list MUST be audit-confirmed to already include `OWASP LLM10:2025` (verified at PRD time + plan time: `[OWASP LLM10:2025, OWASP LLM03:2025]`); zero net change to the metadata block.
- **FR-005**: The system MUST append two new Pattern Categories to `.claude/skills/tachi-denial-of-service/references/detection-patterns.md` after existing Pattern Category 11: **Pattern Category 12 — LLM Inference-Request Flooding and Token Exhaustion** and **Pattern Category 13 — Context-Window Exhaustion (Latency-Driven Variant — Q1 SPLIT Vector A)**. Existing Pattern Categories 1–11 (with the file's hybrid heading structure: 8 thematic groupings for Cat 1–8 + 3 named `## Pattern Category 9/10/11` headings + `## Overview` + `## Targeted DFD Element Types` + `## Primary Sources`) MUST remain byte-identical pre/post edit per ADR-023 Decision 3 (grep-checkable additive-only invariant). This companion does NOT have a `## Trigger Keywords` section (asymmetric to `model-theft` companion).
- **FR-006**: Pattern Category 12 MUST carry: (a) ≥4 indicators covering inference endpoint without per-tenant queries-per-second rate limit, unbounded prompt-size accepted at request ingestion, missing token-budget per request, missing token-counting middleware between request ingestion and model invocation, request-timeout enforcement that does not distinguish LLM-call latency tail-risk from generic HTTP timeouts; (b) ≥1 worked example demonstrating an LLM-API-gateway inference-flooding surface; (c) primary-source citation `OWASP LLM10:2025 — Unbounded Consumption`; (d) related-source citations `CWE-400 Uncontrolled Resource Consumption` and `CWE-770 Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling`; (e) named LLM-specific mitigations including per-tenant queries-per-second rate limit at the API gateway, prompt-size cap (max-prompt-token enforcement), per-tenant token budget per request with hard-cap enforcement, request-timeout tuned to LLM-call p99 latency, token-counting middleware with anomaly alerting on per-tenant token-velocity spikes.
- **FR-007**: Pattern Category 13 MUST carry: (a) ≥4 indicators covering adversarial prompt expansion (no max-context-window enforcement at the API gateway), multi-message conversation history without per-conversation truncation policy, recursive prompt patterns not detected at the API gateway, context-window monitoring absent at the inference-runtime layer, per-tenant context-window cap not differentiated from per-request context-window cap; (b) ≥1 worked example demonstrating an adversarial context-window-expansion latency-DoS surface; (c) primary-source citation `OWASP LLM10:2025 — Unbounded Consumption`; (d) related-source citation `CWE-400 Uncontrolled Resource Consumption`; (e) named LLM-specific mitigations including max-context-window enforcement at the API gateway with automatic 413-response on overflow, per-conversation truncation policy with sliding-window limit, recursive-prompt-pattern detection, context-window monitoring with anomaly alerting, per-tenant context-window cap distinct from per-request context-window cap; (f) explicit Q1 SPLIT scope note clarifying Cat 13 covers Vector A (availability disruption) only — Vector B (economic damage) lives in `model-theft` Cat 11.
- **FR-008**: The system MUST append two new Pattern Categories to `.claude/skills/tachi-model-theft/references/detection-patterns.md` after existing Pattern Category 9: **Pattern Category 10 — Cost Amplification via Recursive or Cost-Asymmetric Prompting** and **Pattern Category 11 — Denial-of-Wallet via Context-Window Cost Amplification (Q1 SPLIT Vector B + broader denial-of-wallet attack class)**. Existing Pattern Categories 1–9 plus the file's `## Overview`, `## Targeted DFD Element Types`, and `## Trigger Keywords` (line 19) sections MUST remain byte-identical pre/post edit per ADR-023 Decision 3 (grep-checkable additive-only invariant).
- **FR-009**: Pattern Category 10 MUST carry: (a) ≥4 indicators covering LLM-serving Process accepts recursive or chain-of-thought prompt patterns without depth limit, output-token cap missing or set higher than realistic per-query response length, output-amplification ratio not monitored per query, cost-per-query p99 alerting absent, per-tenant cost-amplification anomaly detection missing; (b) ≥1 worked example demonstrating a recursive-prompt cost-amplification attack; (c) primary-source citation `OWASP LLM10:2025 — Unbounded Consumption`; (d) related-source citation `OWASP LLM03:2025 — Supply Chain` (inherited from agent's existing `owasp_references`); (e) named LLM-specific mitigations including per-query output-token cap, recursive-prompt depth limit, output-amplification-ratio monitoring, cost-per-query p99 alerting tied to per-tenant billing attribution, per-tenant cost-amplification anomaly detection; (f) **MITRE ATT&CK T1496 Resource Hijacking** named in the mitigation narrative as a text-only cross-reference (NOT in `references` because T1496 is not catalog-resolvable).
- **FR-010**: Pattern Category 11 MUST carry: (a) ≥4 indicators covering multi-tenant LLM-serving architecture without per-tenant token budget hard-cap, context-window expansion not capped per-tenant, cost-per-query p99 alerting absent, denial-of-wallet anomaly detection (cost-velocity monitoring) absent, automated tenant suspension on budget breach missing, per-tenant billing attribution missing or computed asynchronously; (b) ≥1 worked example demonstrating a multi-tenant denial-of-wallet attack; (c) primary-source citation `OWASP LLM10:2025 — Unbounded Consumption`; (d) related-source citation `OWASP LLM03:2025 — Supply Chain` (inherited; cost-flow-through-third-party-models adjacency); (e) named LLM-specific mitigations including per-tenant token budget hard-cap at the API gateway, per-tenant context-window cost reconciliation, cost-per-query p99 alerting, denial-of-wallet anomaly detection via cost-velocity monitoring, automated tenant suspension on budget breach, per-tenant billing attribution computed at-query-time; (f) explicit severity-floor note encoding the Q3 RESOLVED 2-condition CRITICAL floor (multi-tenant freemium structure structurally evident AND both per-tenant token budget AND cost alerting absent); HIGH default otherwise; (g) **MITRE ATT&CK T1496 Resource Hijacking** named in the mitigation narrative as a text-only cross-reference.
- **FR-011**: The system MUST extend the `## Primary Sources` list in `.claude/skills/tachi-denial-of-service/references/detection-patterns.md` AND `.claude/skills/tachi-model-theft/references/detection-patterns.md` with `OWASP LLM10:2025 — Unbounded Consumption` as a new entry in each. Existing Primary Sources entries in both companions MUST be preserved byte-identically.
- **FR-012**: The system MUST ship a public per-feature ADR at `docs/architecture/02_ADRs/ADR-034-llm10-unbounded-consumption-audit.md` (next-available ADR number; PRD-time + plan-time verified ADR-033 highest) under the Proposed → Accepted dual-commit pattern (ADR-027/028/029/030/031/032/033 precedent). ADR body MUST include: (a) the Heuristic A enrichment decision at two-agent scope with rationale (signal-class identity with both `denial-of-service` infrastructure-resource-exhaustion sub-class AND `model-theft` extraction-driven-resource-abuse sub-class; consolidation prevents 3-way fragmentation); (b) the additive-only edit discipline per ADR-023 Decision 3 with byte-identity proof on Cat 1–11 (DoS) and Cat 1–9 (model-theft); (c) the **canonical 5-row sub-pattern → owning-agent mapping table** with severity-hint annotation column per Q3 (audit deliverable); (d) the explicit non-creation of a new `unbounded-consumption` agent and rationale; (e) the no-schema-bump asymmetry to F-1/F-2/F-4 and structural symmetry with F-3 (ADR-032 lines 84 and 182 forecast); (f) zero-MAESTRO-reference invariant proof on the four enriched files; (g) cross-references to ADR-021 (byte-identity baseline), ADR-023 (lean+additive pattern), ADR-027 (taxonomy crosswalk), ADR-028 (source attribution schema), ADR-030 D1 (signal-class taxonomy), ADR-031 D8 (regex-alternation rule asymmetry — F-5 does not invoke), ADR-032 (first enrichment-branch execution at single-agent scope; explicit cross-reference to lines 84 + 182 forecast), ADR-033 D2 (sub-scope carve-up structural sibling); (h) Revision History table tracking Proposed → Accepted dates with post-merge SHA fill; (i) Detection Calibration Note clarifying structural-absence detection style consistent with F-1 / F-2 / F-4; (j) Pattern Category Disambiguation subsection formalizing DoS Cat 9 vs. Cat 12/13 and model-theft Cat 6 vs. Cat 10/11 boundaries; (k) no Layer 2 / tachi Cloud / commercial framing — public ADR omits SDR-001 cross-reference per Option C governance.
- **FR-013**: The system MUST regenerate `examples/agentic-app/` (PRD Q5 RESOLVED at PRD time per architect adjudication; multi-component LLM topology already exercised by F-3). The regenerated example MUST surface ≥1 new `D-{N}` finding attributable to Cat 12 OR Cat 13 with concrete LLM-specific mitigations and `OWASP LLM10:2025` in its prose-level `references:` array AND ≥1 new `LLM-{N}` finding attributable to Cat 10 OR Cat 11 with concrete LLM-specific mitigations and `OWASP LLM10:2025` in its prose-level `references:` array.
- **FR-014**: All four enriched files (`denial-of-service.md`, `model-theft.md`, both companion `detection-patterns.md` files) MUST contain **zero MAESTRO references** post-edit — grep-checkable invariant per ADR-023 Decision 2. PRD-time + plan-time baseline: zero MAESTRO references in all four files.
- **FR-015**: The two new Pattern Categories per host MUST enforce a **LLM-serving topology gate**: Cat 12/13 (DoS) and Cat 10/11 (model-theft) emit findings only when the architecture additionally exhibits LLM-serving indicators (declared inference endpoint, LLM API gateway, per-tenant API key, token-counting middleware, cost-attribution layer, multi-tenant LLM-serving topology). Architectures with no LLM-serving topology MUST emit **zero** new Cat 12/13 + Cat 10/11 findings — Cat 1–11 (DoS) and Cat 1–9 (model-theft) fire as they do today. Matches the zero-speculation discipline established by F-1 / F-2 / F-3 / F-4.
- **FR-016**: The system MUST NOT bump `schemas/finding.yaml`. Schema version remains `"1.8"` post-F-5 (PRD-time + plan-time verified post-F-4 baseline). The `id.pattern` regex remains `^(S|T|R|I|D|E|AG|LLM|AGP|OI|MI|TE)-\d+$` — no new prefix; `D` and `LLM` already enumerated. F-5 is the **second BLP-01 detection feature with no schema bump** (after F-3) and the first at two-agent enrichment scope. ADR-032 lines 84 and 182 explicitly forecast this outcome.
- **FR-017**: The system MUST NOT edit `.claude/skills/tachi-shared/references/finding-format-shared.md`. PRD-time + plan-time verified: `denial-of-service` is at line 12 and `model-theft` is at line 16 in the consumers list. F-5 reuses both existing consumer registrations.
- **FR-018**: The system MUST NOT edit any of the **24 detection-tier files** other than the four F-5 enrichment targets. Detection-tier inventory post-F-4 is **28 files** (22 original detection-tier files + F-1's 2 new + F-2's 2 new + F-3's 0 new (enrichment-branch) + F-4's 2 new = 26 plus F-3 enrichment-modified targets remain in-set, totaling 28). F-5 modifies 4 of those 28 (2 host agents + 2 companions); the remaining **24 detection-tier files** stay byte-identical (grep-auditable zero-edit invariant). The 24 files are: 12 other threat-agent files at `.claude/agents/tachi/{spoofing,tampering,repudiation,info-disclosure,privilege-escalation,prompt-injection,data-poisoning,agent-autonomy,tool-abuse,output-integrity,misinformation,human-trust-exploitation}.md` plus their 12 companion `detection-patterns.md` references under `.claude/skills/tachi-{spoofing,tampering,repudiation,info-disclosure,privilege-escalation,prompt-injection,data-poisoning,agent-autonomy,tool-abuse,output-integrity,misinformation,human-trust-exploitation}/references/`.
- **FR-019**: The system MUST NOT functionally edit the orchestrator dispatch tier. PRD-time + plan-time verified: both `denial-of-service` and `model-theft` are already registered across multiple callsites in `.claude/agents/tachi/orchestrator.md` (lines 37, 42, 298, 372) and `.claude/skills/tachi-orchestration/references/dispatch-rules.md` (lines 73, 109, 157). **Annotation carve-out**: a cosmetic one-token annotation update to `dispatch-rules.md` (extending the `denial-of-service` entry to cite LLM10 for parity with the existing `model-theft (OWASP LLM10:2025)` annotation at line 73) is permitted contingent on architect adjudication of PRD Q2 at plan time — this is documentation-only, no functional dispatch change, and does not invalidate the zero-functional-touch claim. Default-NO.
- **FR-020**: The system MUST NOT extend `source_attribution` populator wiring on either host agent. Per PRD-time grep, neither `denial-of-service.md` nor `model-theft.md` currently emit `source_attribution` (the `D-{N}` and `LLM-{N}` populator wiring is deferred to F-A3 per `schemas/finding.yaml` lines 230–238 and ADR-028 Decision 6). F-5 cites LLM10 in the prose-level `references:` array (an existing finding-YAML field present since v1.0). When F-A3 ships, the LLM10 citations established by F-5 will flow into `source_attribution` naturally because the catalog references are already correct. **F-A3 dependency direction is one-way (F-5 → F-A3 inheritance); F-5 does not require F-A3 to ship and does not block on it.**
- **FR-021**: The system MUST NOT edit infrastructure-tier consumer agents (`risk-scorer`, `control-analyzer`, `threat-report`, `threat-infographic`, `report-assembler`). These read `category: denial-of-service` and `category: llm` (existing enum values); new Cat 12/13 + Cat 10/11 findings flow through them without consumer-side changes using the same code paths that already process Cat 1–11 (DoS) and Cat 1–9 (model-theft) findings. The system MUST NOT add new runtime dependencies — `pyproject.toml`, `requirements*.txt`, `package.json` diffs MUST be empty. No new developer dependencies (`pyyaml`, `pytest` already declared).
- **FR-022**: ADR-034 MUST land as Proposed at the start of the build phase to lock the Heuristic A enrichment decision at two-agent scope before pattern-catalog authoring begins; MUST transition Accepted at PR merge with provisional merge-date; MUST post-merge SHA fill recording the squash commit (ADR-027/028/029/030/031/032/033 precedent). Plan stage finalizes the exact wave/slot for the Proposed commit. The PR title MUST conform to Conventional Commits with `feat(229):` prefix so release-please fires on merge. Recommended title (architect/team-lead may refine at plan time): `feat(229): llm10 unbounded consumption verification` or similar Conventional Commit form ≤70 chars. The `/aod.deliver` stage re-verifies pre-merge and recovers via empty release-marker commit if release-please skips post-merge per F-212 incident precedent.

### Key Entities

- **`denial-of-service` agent** (`.claude/agents/tachi/denial-of-service.md`) — existing STRIDE-tier threat detection agent (53 lines pre-edit, ≤120-line cap). F-5 enriches metadata `owasp_references` (one-line append), `## Purpose` (1–3 line extension), and Detection Workflow Step 5 references (LLM10 exemplar mention). Outputs `D-{N}` findings.
- **`model-theft` agent** (`.claude/agents/tachi/model-theft.md`) — existing AI-tier threat detection agent (95 lines pre-edit, ≤150-line cap). F-5 audit-confirms `owasp_references` already includes LLM10 (zero net change) and extends `## Purpose` (1–3 line extension). Outputs `LLM-{N}` findings.
- **`tachi-denial-of-service` skill** (`.claude/skills/tachi-denial-of-service/`) — existing companion skill directory. F-5 enriches `references/detection-patterns.md` (179 lines pre-edit, hybrid heading structure) with two new Pattern Categories (12 + 13) and one new Primary Sources entry.
- **`tachi-model-theft` skill** (`.claude/skills/tachi-model-theft/`) — existing companion skill directory. F-5 enriches `references/detection-patterns.md` (154 lines pre-edit, uniform `## Pattern Category` heading structure) with two new Pattern Categories (10 + 11) and one new Primary Sources entry.
- **`D-{N}` finding** — existing finding-ID prefix for denial-of-service findings. Single-namespace ID space across all 13 categories (1–11 existing + 12–13 new); sequential numbering.
- **`LLM-{N}` finding** — existing finding-ID prefix for model-theft findings. Single-namespace ID space across all 11 categories (1–9 existing + 10–11 new); sequential numbering.
- **Pattern Category 12 — LLM Inference-Request Flooding and Token Exhaustion** — new pattern category in DoS companion covering inference endpoints without per-tenant rate limits, unbounded prompt-size, missing token-budget enforcement. Indicators ≥4. Primary: OWASP LLM10:2025. Related: CWE-400, CWE-770.
- **Pattern Category 13 — Context-Window Exhaustion (Latency-Driven Variant — Q1 SPLIT Vector A)** — new pattern category in DoS companion covering adversarial context-window expansion driving per-request latency to per-tenant timeout (availability disruption). Indicators ≥4. Primary: OWASP LLM10:2025. Related: CWE-400.
- **Pattern Category 10 — Cost Amplification via Recursive or Cost-Asymmetric Prompting** — new pattern category in model-theft companion covering recursive prompts, output-asymmetric queries, output-token caps misconfigured. Indicators ≥4. Primary: OWASP LLM10:2025. Related: OWASP LLM03:2025. T1496 named in mitigation prose only.
- **Pattern Category 11 — Denial-of-Wallet via Context-Window Cost Amplification (Q1 SPLIT Vector B + broader DoW)** — new pattern category in model-theft companion covering multi-tenant denial-of-wallet attacks where the bill is the attack target (economic damage). Indicators ≥4. Primary: OWASP LLM10:2025. Related: OWASP LLM03:2025. T1496 named in mitigation prose only. Severity HIGH default; CRITICAL floor on 2-condition rule (multi-tenant freemium AND both per-tenant token budget AND cost alerting absent).
- **ADR-034** (`docs/architecture/02_ADRs/ADR-034-llm10-unbounded-consumption-audit.md`) — public per-feature ADR documenting the Heuristic A enrichment pattern at two-agent scope as the operational precedent. Authored under dual-commit Proposed → Accepted protocol. Contains the **canonical 5-row sub-pattern → owning-agent mapping table** as the audit deliverable. Public-only; SDR-001 cross-reference lives in private companion docs per Option C governance.
- **LLM-serving topology gate** — detection workflow invariant requiring LLM-serving indicators (declared inference endpoint, LLM API gateway, per-tenant API key, token-counting middleware, cost-attribution layer, multi-tenant LLM-serving topology) before Cat 12/13 + Cat 10/11 finding emission. Non-LLM-serving architectures emit zero new findings.
- **Pattern Category Disambiguation** — explicit non-overlap contracts: (a) DoS Cat 9 (CWE Top 25 generic) vs. Cat 12/13 (LLM-tier) — same CWE-400 root cause but distinct mitigation surfaces; (b) model-theft Cat 6 (Unbounded Inference Consumption pre-existing) vs. Cat 10 (cost-amplification specific vectors) vs. Cat 11 (denial-of-wallet named economic class). Documented in ADR-034 Decision subsections.
- **Q1 SPLIT — context-window exhaustion vector decomposition** — bifurcation of context-window exhaustion into Vector A (latency-DoS, owner DoS Cat 13) and Vector B (cost-DoW, owner model-theft Cat 11). Same architecture surfaces both; neither is a duplicate; ADR-034 audit table assigns each vector to exactly one owning category. F-5 is the first BLP-01 sub-pattern with cross-agent vector decomposition.

---

## Success Criteria *(mandatory)*

### Measurable Outcomes

- **SC-001**: `.claude/agents/tachi/denial-of-service.md` `owasp_references` list includes `OWASP LLM10:2025 — Unbounded Consumption` as the 10th entry; pre-existing 9 entries are preserved byte-identically. Verifiable via grep against the metadata YAML block.
- **SC-002**: `.claude/agents/tachi/denial-of-service.md` post-edit line count is **≤120 lines** (STRIDE tier cap per ADR-023); PRD-time + plan-time baseline 53 lines; expected post-edit 56–60 lines. Verifiable via `wc -l`.
- **SC-003**: `.claude/agents/tachi/denial-of-service.md` `## Purpose` section includes a 1–3 line extension naming the LLM-inference-exhaustion surface; the pre-existing `## Purpose` prose paragraph is byte-identical to its PRD-time state. Verifiable via structural diff of the section excluding the appended lines.
- **SC-004**: `.claude/agents/tachi/denial-of-service.md` Detection Workflow Step 5 references list includes `OWASP LLM10:2025` exemplar mention; pre-existing entries are preserved byte-identically. Verifiable via grep against Step 5.
- **SC-005**: `.claude/agents/tachi/model-theft.md` post-edit line count is **≤150 lines** (AI tier cap per ADR-023); PRD-time + plan-time baseline 95 lines; expected post-edit 98–102 lines. Verifiable via `wc -l`.
- **SC-006**: `.claude/agents/tachi/model-theft.md` `## Purpose` section includes a 1–3 line extension naming the cost-amplification and denial-of-wallet surface; the pre-existing `## Purpose` prose paragraph is byte-identical to its PRD-time state. Verifiable via structural diff. The `owasp_references` list is audit-confirmed to already include `OWASP LLM10:2025`; zero net change to the metadata block (verified via `diff` returning empty on the `owasp_references` block).
- **SC-007**: `.claude/skills/tachi-denial-of-service/references/detection-patterns.md` ships **Pattern Category 12 — LLM Inference-Request Flooding and Token Exhaustion** and **Pattern Category 13 — Context-Window Exhaustion (Latency-Driven Variant)** appended after Cat 11. Each new category carries ≥4 indicators, ≥1 worked example, and named LLM-specific mitigations. Verifiable via section-header grep + per-category content inspection.
- **SC-008**: `.claude/skills/tachi-denial-of-service/references/detection-patterns.md` Pattern Categories 1–11 (with the hybrid heading structure: 8 thematic groupings for Cat 1–8 + 3 named `## Pattern Category 9/10/11` headings) plus the file's `## Overview`, `## Targeted DFD Element Types`, and `## Primary Sources` sections (excluding the additive `OWASP LLM10:2025` Primary Sources entry per FR-011) are **byte-identical pre/post edit** per ADR-023 Decision 3. Verifiable via structural diff returning empty for the unchanged regions (additive-only invariant). This companion does NOT have a `## Trigger Keywords` section (asymmetric to model-theft companion).
- **SC-009**: `.claude/skills/tachi-model-theft/references/detection-patterns.md` ships **Pattern Category 10 — Cost Amplification via Recursive or Cost-Asymmetric Prompting** and **Pattern Category 11 — Denial-of-Wallet via Context-Window Cost Amplification** appended after Cat 9. Each new category carries ≥4 indicators, ≥1 worked example, and named LLM-specific mitigations. Cat 10 + Cat 11 mitigation narratives include text-only `MITRE ATT&CK T1496 Resource Hijacking` cross-reference. Verifiable via section-header grep + per-category content inspection.
- **SC-010**: `.claude/skills/tachi-model-theft/references/detection-patterns.md` Pattern Categories 1–9 plus the file's `## Overview`, `## Targeted DFD Element Types`, `## Trigger Keywords` (line 19), and `## Primary Sources` sections (excluding the additive `OWASP LLM10:2025` Primary Sources entry per FR-011) are **byte-identical pre/post edit** per ADR-023 Decision 3. Verifiable via structural diff returning empty for the unchanged regions.
- **SC-011**: Both companion `## Primary Sources` lists include `OWASP LLM10:2025 — Unbounded Consumption` as a new entry; existing Primary Sources entries in both companions are preserved byte-identically.
- **SC-012**: Public ADR-034 (or next-available ADR number assigned at plan time) is committed at `docs/architecture/02_ADRs/ADR-034-llm10-unbounded-consumption-audit.md` documenting the Heuristic A enrichment decision at two-agent scope, the canonical 5-row sub-pattern → owning-agent mapping table with severity-hint annotation column per Q3, additive-only edit discipline conformance, explicit non-creation of a new `unbounded-consumption` agent, zero-MAESTRO-reference invariant proof on four enriched files, and ADR cross-references (ADR-021, ADR-023, ADR-027, ADR-028, ADR-030 D1, ADR-031 D8 asymmetry, ADR-032 lines 84+182 forecast, ADR-033 D2 sibling). Authored under dual-commit Proposed → Accepted pattern with Revision History table tracking dates and post-merge SHA fill.
- **SC-013**: Orchestrator dispatch invokes both `denial-of-service` and `model-theft` on architectures matching their existing dispatch trigger keywords (no change to dispatch path). The LLM-serving topology gate (FR-015) ensures Cat 12/13 + Cat 10/11 emit ≥1 new `D-{N}` finding AND ≥1 new `LLM-{N}` finding on the regenerated `examples/agentic-app/`. Architectures with no LLM-serving topology emit zero new Cat 12/13 + Cat 10/11 findings — both agents self-gate emission to prevent speculation.
- **SC-014**: All **6 non-LLM-serving example PDF baselines** (`web-app`, `microservices`, `ascii-web-api`, `mermaid-agentic-app`, `free-text-microservice`, `maestro-reference`) regenerate **byte-identically** under `SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=1700000000` per ADR-021 (zero-impact-when-absent invariant — the LLM-serving topology gate guarantees zero Cat 12/13 + Cat 10/11 findings on these baselines). The 7th baseline (`agentic-app`) is the F-5 mutation candidate per FR-013. **Owner**: SC-014 byte-identity verification (6 baseline regen + grep checks) is explicitly assigned to the `tester` agent (separate from `senior-backend-engineer` who authors the edits) — mirrors F-3 / F-4 precedent.
- **SC-015**: Example regeneration on `examples/agentic-app/` (Q5 RESOLVED at PRD time) surfaces ≥1 new `D-{N}` finding attributable to Pattern Category 12 OR 13 with concrete LLM-specific mitigations and `OWASP LLM10:2025` in its prose-level `references:` array AND ≥1 new `LLM-{N}` finding attributable to Pattern Category 10 OR 11 with concrete LLM-specific mitigations and `OWASP LLM10:2025` in its prose-level `references:` array.
- **SC-016**: Zero new runtime dependencies — empty diff on `pyproject.toml`, `requirements*.txt`, `package.json`. Zero new developer dependencies.
- **SC-017**: **24-file zero-edit invariant preserved** on every detection-tier file other than the four F-5 enrichment targets. Detection-tier post-F-4 inventory is **28 files** (22 original + F-1's 2 + F-2's 2 + F-3's 0 net new + F-4's 2 = 26 plus F-3 enrichment-modified targets remain in-set, totaling 28). F-5 modifies 4 files; the remaining 24 stay byte-identical (grep-auditable). Specifically: zero edits to the 12 other threat-agent files at `.claude/agents/tachi/{spoofing,tampering,repudiation,info-disclosure,privilege-escalation,prompt-injection,data-poisoning,agent-autonomy,tool-abuse,output-integrity,misinformation,human-trust-exploitation}.md` or their 12 companion `detection-patterns.md` reference files.
- **SC-018**: **Schema invariant preserved** — `schemas/finding.yaml` `schema_version` remains `"1.8"` post-F-5 (PRD-time + plan-time verified post-F-4 baseline). The `id.pattern` regex remains `^(S|T|R|I|D|E|AG|LLM|AGP|OI|MI|TE)-\d+$` — no new prefix; `D` and `LLM` already enumerated. F-5 is the second BLP-01 detection feature with no schema bump (after F-3) and the first at two-agent enrichment scope. Verifiable via empty diff on `schemas/finding.yaml`.
- **SC-019**: Every emitted Cat 12/13 (`D-{N}`) and Cat 10/11 (`LLM-{N}`) finding's prose-level `references:` array carries OWASP LLM10:2025 at minimum. Each new `D-{N}` finding's `references` includes `OWASP LLM10:2025`, `CWE-400 Uncontrolled Resource Consumption`, and where applicable `CWE-770 Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling`. Each new `LLM-{N}` finding's `references` includes `OWASP LLM10:2025`, and where applicable `OWASP LLM03:2025` for the cost-flow-through-third-party-models adjacency. **MITRE ATT&CK T1496 Resource Hijacking** is named in finding `mitigation` narratives on Cat 10/11 as a text-only cross-reference but does NOT appear as a structured `references` entry (T1496 not catalog-resolvable). All catalog-cited IDs (LLM10, CWE-400, CWE-770, LLM03) are verified present in `schemas/taxonomy/{owasp,cwe}.yaml`. F-5 does NOT extend `source_attribution` populator wiring (deferred to F-A3).
- **SC-020**: All four enriched files (`denial-of-service.md`, `model-theft.md`, both companion `detection-patterns.md`) contain **zero MAESTRO references** — verified via `grep -i 'maestro' <files>` returning no matches. PRD-time + plan-time baseline: zero MAESTRO references in all four files; F-5 preserves this invariant.
- **SC-021**: `.claude/agents/tachi/orchestrator.md` and `.claude/skills/tachi-orchestration/references/dispatch-rules.md` receive **zero functional edits** in F-5. An optional cosmetic annotation carve-out (extending the `denial-of-service` entry in `dispatch-rules.md` to cite LLM10 for parity with the existing `model-theft (OWASP LLM10:2025)` annotation at line 73) is permitted contingent on architect plan-day adjudication of PRD Q2; if applied, it is documentation-only and does not constitute a functional dispatch change. Default-NO. BLP-01 Coverage Matrix updated post-merge: LLM10:2025 transitions Partial → Covered with F-5 (Feature 229) named as the closure feature. OWASP LLM Top 10:2025 framework coverage advances to **10/10 Covered** (full LLM Top 10 closure milestone). Combined with F-4's ASI09 closure: **20/20 OWASP AI top-10 entries Covered**.
- **SC-022**: PR title is `feat(229): llm10 unbounded consumption verification` (or similar Conventional Commit form ≤70 chars beginning with `feat(229):`). Pre-merge re-verification at `/aod.deliver` confirms the title; post-merge release-please PR opens within ~30s. If release-please skips, recovery via empty `feat(229): ... release marker` commit per F-212 incident precedent. **Delivery retrospective filed** at `specs/229-llm10-unbounded-consumption-verification/delivery.md` (mirrors F-1 + F-2 + F-3 + F-4 precedent) capturing actual vs. estimated effort, **second execution of Heuristic A enrichment branch at two-agent scope** lessons (precedent for F-6/F-7 Tier 2 ML+Mobile bundles), Q1 SPLIT cross-agent vector decomposition lessons, byte-identity preservation evidence (SC-008 + SC-010 + SC-014 grep proofs across 6 baselines), canonical sub-pattern mapping table audit-deliverable lessons, ADR-034 Accepted-commit SHA-fill execution, and any deviations from PRD timeline or scope. Authored same-day-as-delivery if Day 1 PM merge has ≥1 hour residual capacity; otherwise authored on Buffer Day 2 (2026-04-30 Thursday).

---

## Out of Scope (Explicit)

The following items are explicitly out of scope for F-5. Each carries a forward-reference per PRD scope boundaries:

1. **New `unbounded-consumption` agent** → explicitly REJECTED per Heuristic A consolidation; SDR-001 Decision 4 locked the resolution. Reopening this would re-adjudicate every prior Heuristic A consolidation (F-1 LLM05 → output-integrity, F-2 LLM09 → misinformation, F-3 ASI07 → tool-abuse enrichment, F-4 ASI09 → human-trust-exploitation + agent-autonomy carve-up).
2. **New `denial-of-wallet` standalone agent** → explicitly REJECTED per same Heuristic A reasoning; denial-of-wallet is the named attack class for `model-theft` Pattern Category 11.
3. **Schema bump 1.8 → 1.9** → F-5 reuses the existing `D-{N}` and `LLM-{N}` prefixes; no new ID prefix introduced. F-5 is the second BLP-01 detection feature without a schema bump (after F-3) and the first at two-agent enrichment scope.
4. **`finding-format-shared.md` consumers list edit** → both `denial-of-service` (line 12) and `model-theft` (line 16) already present (PRD-time + plan-time verified). No edit required.
5. **Functional orchestrator/dispatch-rules edits** → both agents already fully registered (PRD-time + plan-time verified across multiple callsites). Only optional cosmetic annotation carve-out per PRD Q2 (architect plan-day decision, default-NO).
6. **`source_attribution` populator wiring on the host agents** → deferred to F-A3 per `schemas/finding.yaml` lines 230–238 and ADR-028 Decision 6. F-5 cites LLM10 in prose-level `references:` array only. F-A3 dependency is one-way (F-5 → F-A3 inheritance).
7. **MITRE ATT&CK T1496 catalog population** → out-of-scope for F-5; cited in mitigation prose only. Future catalog-enrichment features may upgrade this to a structured `references` entry.
8. **OWASP ML Top 10 bundle (F-6)** → Tier 2 follow-on, deferred per PRD §BLP-01 Initiative Fit. The Heuristic A enrichment-branch at two-agent scope precedent F-5 establishes informs but does not block F-6.
9. **OWASP Mobile Top 10 bundle (F-7)** → Tier 2 follow-on, deferred per PRD §BLP-01 Initiative Fit.
10. **Web/API attestation (F-8)** → Tier 3, sequenced last.
11. **Cross-feature attack-chain reasoning** → Feature 141 cross-layer attack chains already covers this; new Cat 12/13 + Cat 10/11 findings flow through Phase 3.5 chain correlation automatically with zero F-5 scope.
12. **Catalog population for additional MITRE ATT&CK or CWE IDs** → F-5 cites only IDs verified present at PRD time + plan time (LLM10, LLM03, CWE-400, CWE-770). T1496 is named in mitigation prose only.
13. **Commercial framing in ADR-034** → Layer 2, tachi Cloud, enterprise-only features explicitly excluded per public-ADR governance (Option C contract from SDR-001). Public ADR stands on technical merits only.
14. **New baselines** → F-5 reuses the existing 6-baseline byte-identity matrix (`web-app`, `microservices`, `ascii-web-api`, `mermaid-agentic-app`, `free-text-microservice`, `maestro-reference`) plus `agentic-app` mutation target.
15. **Severity rule re-calibration post-adopter feedback** → R5 mitigation: severity rule is a one-line edit to Cat 11 worked example or ADR-034 audit-table annotation column; not an F-5 scope expansion.
16. **Non-LLM10 sub-patterns** → F-5 closes LLM10:2025 specifically; LLM01-LLM09 closures are already complete (F-1 closed LLM05, F-2 closed LLM09; LLM01-04, LLM06-08 closed by pre-BLP-01 work).

---

## Assumptions

- The architect confirms Heuristic A enrichment-vs-new-agent distinctness is intact at two-agent scope (SDR-001 Decision 4 locked the resolution; ADR-030 + ADR-032 reinforced the signal-class taxonomy in the LLM tier; F-3 demonstrated the enrichment branch within the AG tier; F-5 demonstrates it within the LLM tier at two-agent scope) at the start of Day 1 build and commits ADR-034 Proposed at the build-start lock wave. If a subsume-into-new-agent signal surfaces during pattern authoring, escalation to architect is required before Day 1 EOD (per PRD R2 Day 1 gate).
- `examples/agentic-app/` is the regeneration target per PRD Q5 RESOLVED. No new architecture authoring needed; mutation candidate is the multi-component LLM topology already exercised by F-3. ADR-034 includes a Detection Calibration Note clarifying that the new Pattern Categories 12/13 (DoS) and 10/11 (model-theft) fire on **structural absence** of named control mechanisms — consistent with F-1 / F-2 / F-4 absence-style detection style.
- Pattern Category 12, 13, 10, 11 indicator counts are ≥4 per FR-006/007/009/010 (PRD-time architect-leaning: 5 indicators per Cat 12/13, 5 per Cat 10/11). Architect may add or refine indicators at plan time. Architect may merge into single category per agent (2-category floor per Issue #229 DoD bullet) at plan time per Q4 if the indicator surface justifies — Q4 RESOLVED at PRD time as 4 categories total but architect retains plan-day floor authority.
- Severity baseline per Q3 RESOLVED: Cat 12 + Cat 13 MEDIUM-HIGH default; Cat 10 HIGH default; Cat 11 HIGH default with **CRITICAL floor** ONLY when (a) multi-tenant freemium structure structurally evident in the architecture AND (b) both per-tenant token budget AND cost alerting absent. Encoded as severity-hint annotation column in ADR-034 Decision 3 audit table. Worked example for Cat 11 explicitly states the 2-condition Critical floor.
- Cosmetic dispatch-rules annotation update (extending `denial-of-service` entry in `dispatch-rules.md` to cite LLM10 for parity with model-theft's existing `(OWASP LLM10:2025)` annotation at line 73) is contingent on PRD Q2 architect plan-day adjudication (default-NO; documentation-only, no functional dispatch change). Architect may resolve YES (cosmetic parity) or NO (no annotation update).
- ADR-034 lands as Proposed at the start of the build phase to lock Heuristic A enrichment at two-agent scope before pattern-catalog authoring; transitions to Accepted at PR merge with provisional merge-date and post-merge SHA fill (mirrors ADR-027/028/029/030/031/032/033 precedent). Plan stage finalizes the exact wave/slot.
- ADR-034 numbering is the next available number at merge time (PRD-time + plan-time verified: ADR-033 is highest existing; ADR-034 is next). Team-lead reconfirms at plan time; renumber is a one-line edit if a parallel feature claims ADR-034 first.
- All 4 catalog citations (LLM10, LLM03, CWE-400, CWE-770) are verified present at PRD time + plan time. Architect re-verifies at plan time per PRD R4. F-A2 referential-integrity validator (when F-A3 ships) will pass by construction. T1496 is verified absent in `schemas/taxonomy/mitre-attack.yaml`; named in mitigation prose only.
- F-A1, F-A2, F-B, F-1, F-2, F-3, F-4 dependencies are satisfied at PRD time + plan time (verified: `schemas/taxonomy/` populated with all 4 OWASP/CWE citations, `schemas/finding.yaml` v1.8, both host agents already in `finding-format-shared.md` consumers list, `templates/tachi/security-report/coverage-attestation.typ` exists, F-1 / F-2 / F-3 / F-4 detection agents present, ADR-030/031/032/033 Accepted).
- PM PRD sign-off is APPROVED; architect APPROVED_WITH_CONCERNS (12/12 prior findings RESOLVED in v2 — all 2 BLOCKING / 3 HIGH / 4 MEDIUM / 3 LOW absorbed inline); team-lead APPROVED_WITH_CONCERNS (3 MEDIUM + 2 LOW absorbed inline). Q1 SPLIT / Q3 severity / Q4 4-categories / Q5 example-target PRD-time resolutions preserved as constraint baselines. Architect-tractable Q2 cosmetic dispatch annotation properly deferred to plan time.
- F-5 ships solo or as the first of multiple parallel-eligible features in the 2026-04-29 → 2026-04-30 window (no parallel feature in window per PRD A3). If F-6 / F-7 enter build concurrently after F-5, they will not edit the same files (F-6 / F-7 target different host agents per BLP-01 Tier 2 sequencing).
- Day 2 / Buffer Day (2026-04-30 Thursday) is reserved primarily for byte-identity verification on remaining 4 baselines (per team-lead MEDIUM-1 rebalance; 2 baselines spot-checked at Day 1 PM end), ADR-034 Accepted-transition polish, PR review feedback turnaround, and delivery retrospective filing per DoD bullet 15 + 16. Buffer-day priority order per team-lead MEDIUM-3: (1) Day 1 slip absorption, (2) delivery retrospective, (3) post-merge ADR-034 SHA fill + `/aod.deliver` execution + release-please PR verification, (4) F-6 PRD drafting NOT until F-5 deliver-stage closes.
- The PR title is set to `feat(229): llm10 unbounded consumption verification` (or similar Conventional Commit form ≤70 chars) at draft PR creation per PRD Release Discipline subsection. Title is re-verified pre-merge at `/aod.deliver` and recovery via empty release-marker commit is invoked if release-please skips post-merge per F-212 precedent.
- The Heuristic A enrichment-branch at two-agent scope is the first cross-agent application; F-3 ADR-032 lines 84 and 182 explicitly forecast F-5's no-bump outcome. Plan-day re-verifies the forecast holds at build start.
